title: Castle in the Air - Chapter Five: Which tells how Flower-in-the-Night’s father wished to raise Abdullah above all others in the land (Fragment 1)
text:
Castle in the Air

Chapter Five

W HICH TELLS HOW F LOWER-IN-THE -N IGHT ’ S FATHER WISHED TO RAISE A BDULLAH ABOVE ALL OTHERS IN THE LAND

“What a day!” Abdullah said to himself when he was back inside his booth at last. “If my luck goes on this way, I will not be surprised if I never get the carpet to move again!” Or, he thought as he lay down on the carpet, still dressed in his best, he might get to the night garden only to find that Flower-in-the-Night was too annoyed at his stupidity last night to love him anymore. Or she might love him still but have decided not to fly away with him. Or …

It took him a while to get to sleep.

But when he woke, everything was perfect. The carpet was just gliding to a gentle landing on the moonlit bank. So Abdullah knew he had said the command word after all, and it was such a short while since he had said it that he almost had a memory of what it was. But it went clean out of his head when Flower-in-the-Night came running eagerly toward him, among the white scented flowers and the round yellow lamps.

“You’re here!” she called as she ran. “I was quite worried!”

She was not angry. Abdullah’s heart sang. “Are you ready to leave?” he called back. “Jump on beside me.”

Flower-in-the-Night laughed delightedly—it was definitely no giggle—and came running on across the lawn. The moon seemed just then to go behind a cloud because Abdullah saw her lit entirely by the lamps for a moment, golden and eager, as she ran. He stood up and held out his hands to her.

As he did so, the cloud came right down into the lamplight. And it was not a cloud but great black leathery wings, silently beating. A pair of equally leathery arms, with hands that had long fingernails like claws, reached from the shadow of those fanning wings and wrapped themselves around Flower-in-the-Night. Abdullah saw her jerk as those arms stopped her running. She looked around and up. Whatever she saw made her scream, one single wild, frantic scream, which was cut off when one of the leathery arms changed position to clap its huge taloned hand over her face. Flower-in-the-Night beat at the arm with her fists, and kicked and struggled, but all quite uselessly. She was lifted up, a small white figure against the huge blackness. The great wings silently beat again. A gigantic foot, with talons like the hands, pressed the turf a yard or so from the bank where Abdullah was still in the act of standing up, and a leathery leg flexed mighty calf muscles as the thing—whatever it was—sprang upright. For the merest instant Abdullah found himself staring into a hideous leathery face with a ring through its hooked nose and long, upslanting eyes, remote and cruel. The thing was not looking at him. It was simply concentrating on getting itself and its captive airborne.

The next second it was aloft. Abdullah saw it overhead for a heartbeat longer, a mighty flying djinn dangling a tiny, pale human girl in its arms. Then the night swallowed it up. It all had happened unbelievably quickly.

“After it! Follow that djinn!” Abdullah ordered the carpet.

The carpet seemed to obey. It bellied up from the bank. Then, almost as if someone had given it another command, it sank back and lay still.

“You moth-eaten doormat!” Abdullah screamed at it.

There was a shout from farther down the garden. “This way, men! That scream came from up there!”

Along the arcade Abdullah glimpsed moonlight on metal helmets and—worse still—golden lamplight on swords and crossbows. He did not wait to explain to these people why he had screamed. He flung himself flat on the carpet.

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones
meta.sectionTitle: Chapter Five: Which tells how Flower-in-the-Night’s father wished to raise Abdullah above all others in the land

vocabulary.definition: Up in or into the air; overhead.
vocabulary.example: The djinn was aloft, flying with the captive girl.

vocabulary.definition: A covered passageway with arches along one or both sides.
vocabulary.example: Along the arcade Abdullah glimpsed moonlight on helmets.

vocabulary.definition: To swell or curve outward like a belly.
vocabulary.example: It bellied up from the bank.

vocabulary.definition: To put your hands together quickly or to strike something suddenly with your hand.
vocabulary.example: One of the leathery arms clapped its huge taloned hand over her face.

vocabulary.definition: An order given to control or direct.
vocabulary.example: He had said the command word to move the carpet.

vocabulary.definition: Focusing all attention on a particular task or object.
vocabulary.example: It was simply concentrating on getting itself and its captive airborne.

vocabulary.definition: A weapon consisting of a bow mounted on a stock that shoots arrows or bolts.
vocabulary.example: Golden lamplight on swords and crossbows.

vocabulary.definition: Causing pain or suffering without pity.
vocabulary.example: A face that was remote and cruel.

vocabulary.definition: Hung loosely and swung back and forth.
vocabulary.example: A flying djinn dangling a tiny human girl in its arms.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows great pleasure or enjoyment.
vocabulary.example: Flower-in-the-Night laughed delightedly—it was definitely no giggle.

vocabulary.definition: Moving the air to cool or stir something.
vocabulary.example: The shadow of those fanning wings.

vocabulary.definition: To bend a limb or muscle to tighten it.
vocabulary.example: A leathery leg flexed mighty calf muscles.

vocabulary.definition: Threw oneself or something with force or suddenly.
vocabulary.example: He flung himself flat on the carpet.

vocabulary.definition: Done in a hurried, excited, and disorganized way due to fear or anxiety.
vocabulary.example: She screamed one single wild, frantic scream.

vocabulary.definition: Of very great size or extent; huge.
vocabulary.example: A gigantic foot with talons pressed the turf.

vocabulary.definition: Moving smoothly and continuously along, as if without effort.
vocabulary.example: The carpet was just gliding to a gentle landing on the moonlit bank.

vocabulary.definition: Saw something briefly or partially.
vocabulary.example: He glimpsed moonlight on metal helmets.

vocabulary.definition: Extremely ugly or disgusting.
vocabulary.example: A hideous leathery face with a ring through its hooked nose.

vocabulary.definition: Curved or bent like a hook.
vocabulary.example: Its hooked nose and long, upslanting eyes.

vocabulary.definition: The light that comes from a lamp.
vocabulary.example: A cloud came right down into the lamplight.

vocabulary.definition: Having a tough, thick, and flexible texture like leather.
vocabulary.example: Great black leathery wings, silently beating.

vocabulary.definition: Illuminated by the light of the moon.
vocabulary.example: A gentle landing on the moonlit bank.

vocabulary.definition: To follow commands or instructions.
vocabulary.example: The carpet seemed to obey.

vocabulary.definition: Distant or aloof in manner or feeling.
vocabulary.example: Eyes that were remote and cruel.

vocabulary.definition: Having a smell or fragrance.
vocabulary.example: Among the white scented flowers and the round yellow lamps.

vocabulary.definition: Made forceful or violent efforts to get free or overcome something.
vocabulary.example: She kicked and struggled, but quite uselessly.

vocabulary.definition: Enveloped or engulfed completely.
vocabulary.example: The night swallowed it up.

vocabulary.definition: Having claws or talons, especially referring to hands or feet with sharp curved nails.
vocabulary.example: A huge taloned hand covered her face.

vocabulary.definition: Slanting upwards.
vocabulary.example: Long, upslanting eyes that looked remote and cruel.

vocabulary.definition: Enclosed or covered something entirely with a piece of material.
vocabulary.example: Arms wrapped themselves around Flower-in-the-Night.
